`nditer` does not allow `None`-valued elements in operand sequence","user":{"login":"simonaltrogge","id":8720147,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg3MjAxNDc=","avatar_url":"https://private-avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8720147?jwt=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTEiLCJleHAiOjE3MzQ2MjEzMDAsIm5iZiI6MTczNDYyMDEwMCwicGF0aCI6Ii91Lzg3MjAxNDcifQ.efEeGwoMEAnHqO7c-VPelIgys2i-40kJxiTvgQCpUUA&v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge","html_url":"https://github.com/simonaltrogge","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":2124388074,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyMTI0Mzg4MDc0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/Static%20typing","name":"Static typing","color":"fcff6b","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-12-19T15:08:57Z","updated_at":"2024-12-19T15:08:57Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

Passing `None`-valued elements next to array_like-valued elements in a sequence as operands to `nditer` is part of the documented idiom of [iterator-allocated output arrays](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/arrays.nditer.html#iterator-allocated-output-arrays). Mentions of this can also be found in the [documentation of `nditer`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.nditer.html) directly. The type-hints do not reflect this, however, and lead to an error instead when using this intended way of calling `nditer`.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np
import numpy.typing as npt

def square(a: npt.NDArray):
    with np.nditer([a, None]) as it:
        for x, y in it:
            y[...] = x*x
        return it.operands[1]
```


### Error message:

```shell
Argument of type "list[NDArray[Unknown] | None]" cannot be assigned to parameter "op" of type "ArrayLike | Sequence[ArrayLike]" in function "__new__"
  Type "None" is not assignable to type "ArrayLike"
    "None" is incompatible with protocol "Buffer"
      "__buffer__" is not present
    "None" is incompatible with protocol "_SupportsArray[dtype[Any]]"
      "__array__" is not present
    "None" is incompatible with protocol "_NestedSequence[_SupportsArray[dtype[Any]]]"
      "__len__" is not present
      "__getitem__" is not present Pylance (reportArgumentType)
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

2.2.0
3.13.1 | packaged by conda-forge | (main, Dec  5 2024, 21:23:54) [GCC 13.3.0]

### Type-checker version and settings:

Pylance 2024.12.1 used in Visual Studio Code 1.96.0

### Additional typing packages.

typing","color":"fcff6b","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-12-19T15:08:57Z","updated_at":"2024-12-19T17:30:16Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

Passing `None`-valued elements next to array_like-valued elements in a sequence as operands to `nditer` is part of the documented idiom of [iterator-allocated output arrays](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/arrays.nditer.html#iterator-allocated-output-arrays). Mentions of this can also be found in the [documentation of `nditer`](https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.nditer.html) directly. The type-hints do not reflect this, however, and lead to an error instead when using this intended way of calling `nditer`.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np
import numpy.typing as npt

def square(a: npt.NDArray):
    with np.nditer([a, None]) as it:
        for x, y in it:
            y[...] = x*x
        return it.operands[1]
```


### Error message:

```shell
Argument of type "list[NDArray[Unknown] | None]" cannot be assigned to parameter "op" of type "ArrayLike | Sequence[ArrayLike]" in function "__new__"
  Type "None" is not assignable to type "ArrayLike"
    "None" is incompatible with protocol "Buffer"
      "__buffer__" is not present
    "None" is incompatible with protocol "_SupportsArray[dtype[Any]]"
      "__array__" is not present
    "None" is incompatible with protocol "_NestedSequence[_SupportsArray[dtype[Any]]]"
      "__len__" is not present
      "__getitem__" is not present Pylance (reportArgumentType)
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

2.2.0
3.13.1 | packaged by conda-forge | (main, Dec  5 2024, 21:23:54) [GCC 13.3.0]

### Type-checker version and settings:

Pylance 2024.12.1 used in Visual Studio Code 1.96.0

### Additional typing packages.

{"action":"opened","number":27721,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/pulls/27721","id":2169959559,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAA3dP86BVvSH","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27721","diff_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27721.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/pull/27721.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27721","number":27721,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"DOC: fix name of shape parameter kappa of von Mises distribution","user":{"login":"simonaltrogge","id":8720147,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg3MjAxNDc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8720147?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge","html_url":"https://github.com/simonaltrogge","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simonaltrogge/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"The parameter kappa in the equation of the von Mises distribution is not the dispersion but its reciprocal. This is typically referred to as the concentration (see for example https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.stats.vonmises.html).
